member.name: Mr Richard Holden
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Mr Richard Holden is the Conservative MP for Basildon and Billericay , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019. He currently undertakes the role of Shadow Secretary of State for Transport.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadcasting Programmes: Russia
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether (a) her Department and (b) Ofcom has made an assessment of the potential implications for its polices on foreign interference online of the potential use of the animated televis
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government takes the threat of foreign interference seriously and works across departments, intelligence partners and regulators to identify and respond to state-linked information threats. However, it is not this department’s function to routinely assess...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Hammersmith Bridge: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has made an assessment of the potential impact of the requirement that projects supported through the Structures Fund be completed by 31 March 2030 on the eligibility of Hammersmith Bridge for fundi
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local highway authorities have a duty under Section 41 of the Highways Act 1980 to maintain the highways network in their area. Local highway authorities putting forward structures for consideration for investment from the Structures Fund are responsible ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Structures Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she plans to review the requirement that projects funded through the Structures Fund be completed by 31 March 2030 in cases where that deadline may prevent the restoration of nationally significant tran
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no plans to review this requirement. Eligibility for the Fund is restricted to structures that require repair or replacement now or are likely to before 31 March 2030. Schemes to repair or replace these structures must be completed and reopened ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cement: Industry
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether his Department has made an assessment of the level of importance of the cement industry to the economy and the number of jobs reliant on it.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No specific assessment on the importance of the cement industry to the economy or number of jobs reliant on it has been conducted. However, the Government is aware of the sector's importance to the economy and government priorities as well as the number o...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether his Department holds data on the i.) amount of cement and proportion of the UK's domestic requirement for cement has been produced in the UK in each of the last 10 years ii.) amount of cement a
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Business and Trade does not hold information about either the amount of cement produced in the UK to meet domestic production requirements in the last 10 years, or the amount of cement imported to meet domestic production requirements i...

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Airspace Design Service: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 30 June 2026 to Question 13301 on Air Routes, how many staff the UK Airspace Design Service is expected to employ when fully operational.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Airspace Design Service (UKADS) has commenced work and is completing its mobilisation. It is for NATS En Route plc, as the licence holder, to determine the staffing required to deliver the service and be held accountable for its performance. Once f...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Transport: Written Questions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when she plans to respond to my Question 11459.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Question 11459 was answered on 8 July 2026.

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Transport: Written Questions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when she plans to respond to my Question 11744.
writtenQuestions.answerText: PQ UIN 11744 was answered on 3 July 2026.

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Alternative Fuels
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what estimate she has made of the proportion of the total cost of compliance with the Sustainable Aviation Fuel Mandate represented by the compliance fees charged by sustainable aviation fuel suppliers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Mandate cost-benefit analysis, the total cost of compliance was estimated by looking at the additional cost of supplying SAF compared to kerosene, as well as the change to any carbon price obligation that app...

writtenQuestions.heading: Network Rail: Biodiversity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 13067 on Network Rail: Biodiversity, whether her Department plans to require Network Rail to record centrally the (a) costs and (b) delays to projects arising
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport does not plan to require Network Rail to record centrally the costs and any delays to projects arising from protected species survey, licencing and mitigation requirements. Network Rail must comply with species licencing requi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Greater Manchester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 11461 on Greater Manchester Combined Authority, in which month she expects the work set out in the Compact Agreement of 22 January 2026 to report.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Rt Hon Member to the Answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 11461.

writtenQuestions.heading: Judiciary: Public Appointments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, pursuant to the Answer of 22 June 2026 to Question 8930 on Judicial Appointments, in how many judicial appointments in each year since the coming into force of the equal merit provision in the Crime and Courts Ac
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Equal Merit Provision was introduced in 2014. Where two or more candidates a vacancy are judged by the Judicial Appointments Commission to be of equal merit, the Commission may give preference to the candidates with relevant protected characteristics ...

writtenQuestions.heading: South Western Railway: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 12576 on South Western Railway, what data her Department holds on the punctuality of South Western Railway services since 25 May 2025, broken down by reporting
writtenQuestions.answerText: South Western Railway report Time-to-3 punctuality (the percentage of station stops arrived at within 3 minutes of their scheduled time) to DfT Operator. This data and more performance data is available on the ORR website here: Passenger rail performance ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Transport: Public Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 23 June 2026 to Question 9949 on Active Travel, how much of the projected £3.448 billion from wider funding streams within the Department for Transport and other Government departments
writtenQuestions.answerText: None.

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 24 June 2026 to Question 11454 on Heavy Goods Vehicles, whether the analysis referred to in that Answer has been published.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No, we have not published this analysis yet, but we will set out further details alongside the next consultation on the Heavy Goods Vehicle regulations.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Mandate cost-benefit analysis set out the estimates of the impact on an average one-way ticket price (Sustainable aviation fuel mandate: final stage cost benefit analysis). This analysis assumes that airlines pass on 75...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 11946 on Train Operating Companies: Finance, whether her Department has undertaken analysis on the factors resulting in passenger revenue in 2025-26 being lowe
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department holds regular discussions with DFTO to monitor and review passenger revenue outturn against forecast. Outturn passenger revenue can diverge from forecast for a variety of reasons. These include external factors (such as wider economic condi...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Allowances for 'hardship' locations have been a feature of civil service overseas postings for several decades, and New Delhi has been included in the list of those locations since it was first introduced. The list is reviewed on a regular basis to reflec...

registeredInterests.category: 3. Gifts, benefits and hospitality from UK sources

items.text: Name of donor: London Marathon Events Ltd Address of donor: 190 Great Dover Street, London, England, SE1 4YB Amount of donation or nature and value if donation in kind: Access to tented hospitality area with facilities at the start and finish of the London Marathon, value £350 Date received: 26 April 2026 Date accepted: 26 April 2026 Donor status: company, registration 01528489 (Registered 13 May 2026)

Name of donor: UK Friends of Ukraine Address of donor: 62 Lots Road, London SW10 0QD Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Coach travel from Moldova to Ukraine and within Ukraine (£223.94), Chernobyl Visit (£46.15), meals and sundry items (£88.83), value £350.72 Destination of visit: Ukraine (Odessa, Kyiv, Chernobyl) and Moldova Dates of visit: 20 February 2026 to 25 February 2026 Purpose of visit: Gain a greater understanding of the continuing impact of Russia's illegal invasion of Ukraine and the continuing war. (Registered 18 March 2026)

Name of donor: European Parliamentary Forum Address of donor: Square de Meeus 18, 1050 Brussels, Belgium Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (Economy, £606 and £678), transfers (£100), accommodation (£663) and meals (150), value £2,197 Destination of visit: Zambia Dates of visit: 12 April 2026 to 17 April 2026 Purpose of visit: Study focussing on sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) in Zambia, especially in rural areas and poorer parts of cities, including looking at cooperation and innovation. (Registered 18 May 2026)

Name of donor: Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung Address of donor: 42 Essex Street, London WC2R 3JF Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (Economy £181), transfers (£79.65), accommodation (£310.89) and meals (£88.25), value £659.79 Destination of visit: Italy Dates of visit: 17 April 2026 to 19 April 2026 Purpose of visit: British-German Parliamentary Dialogue with CDU and CSU Members of the German Bundestag. (Registered 18 May 2026)

Name of donor: The Coalition for Global Prosperity (CGP) Address of donor: 1 Horse Guards Avenue, London SW1A 2HU Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (Economy) (£1303.54), accomodation (£843.77), local transport (£1496.14), food & miscellaneous expenses (£434.79), value £4,078.24 Destination of visit: Kenya (Nairobi, Marsabit) Dates of visit: 24 May 2026 to 29 May 2026 Purpose of visit: Cross-party visit to Kenya to engage with political, business and civil society stakeholders on shared UK-Kenya priorities (Registered 8 June 2026)
Executive Committee Member of the British-American Parliamentary Group. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 30 October 2024 (Registered 9 January 2025)
